title: To Me At My Fifth-Floor Window
content:
To me at my fifth-floor window
    The chimney-pots in rows
    Are sets of pipes pandean
    For every wind that blows;

    And the smoke that whirls and eddies
    In a thousand times and keys
    Is really a visible music
    Set to my reveries.

    O monstrous pipes, melodious
    With fitful tune and dream,
    The clouds are your only audience,
    Her thought is your only theme!

    1875
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Ernest Henley, titled "To Me At My Fifth-Floor Window", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
